html:
[2630]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 生生之费无阙焉 - A formal expression meaning 'all expenses for living are without shortage,' indicating a state of abundance or sufficiency.
  • 埴至阙方知感悰 - A somewhat ambiguous phrase. '埴' likely refers to a person named 'Zhi.' '阙' likely means 'to arrive at the palace gate.' '感悰' means 'to feel joy' or 'to experience pleasure.'
  • 寻除光禄卿 - '寻除' means 'shortly after being appointed,' and '光禄卿' is the title of a high-ranking official in the imperial court.
  • 报状至蜀 - '报状' means 'official report,' and '至蜀' means 'arrived in Shu' (the region around modern-day Sichuan).
  • 贵人至阙也 - '贵人' means 'noble person' or 'high-ranking official,' and '至阙' means 'arrived at the palace gate.'
  • 姑待之 - Means 'wait for it' or 'hold on.'
  • 稍进大理卿 - '稍进' means 'gradually advanced,' and '大理卿' is the title of the chief justice.
  • 迁刑部侍郎 - '迁' means 'promoted to,' and '刑部侍郎' is the title of a deputy minister in the Ministry of Justice.
  • 充盐铁使 - '充' means 'to be appointed as,' and '盐铁使' is the title of an official responsible for salt and iron monopolies.
  • 始惊忧 - '始' means 'beginning,' '惊' means 'surprised,' and '忧' means 'worried.'
  • 俄而作相 - '俄而' means 'soon after,' and '作相' means 'became chancellor.'
  • 懿安皇后宣宗幽崩 - '懿安皇后' is the title of the Empress, '宣宗' is the era name of Emperor Xuanzong, and '幽崩' means 'died in seclusion' or 'died mysteriously.'
  • 内牓子索检责宰臣元载故事 - '内牓子' means 'internal edict,' '索检责' means 'investigate and hold accountable,' '宰臣' means 'high-ranking officials,' and '元载故事' means 'the story of Yuan Zai' (a historical figure).
  • 埴喻防 - '埴' is a person's name, '喻防' means 'advised to be cautious.'
  • 延英上前万端营救 - '延英' is a hall in the imperial palace, '上前' means 'in front of the emperor,' and '万端营救' means 'tried every means to save.'
  • 素辩博能廻上意 - '素' means 'usually,' '辩博' means 'eloquent and knowledgeable,' and '廻上意' means 'change the emperor's mind.'
  • 事遂寝 - '事遂寝' means 'the matter was put to rest.'
  • 出前定録 - '出前定録' means 'recorded in the *Qian Ding Lu* (a historical record).'
  • 王锷 - A person's name.
  • 辛杲 - A person's name.
  • 偏禆 - Means 'subordinate' or 'assistant.'
  • 帅长沙 - '帅' means 'commander,' and '长沙' is the name of a city.
  • 击毬驰骋 - '击毬' means 'play ball,' and '驰骋' means 'gallop' or 'ride vigorously.'
  • 向天呵气 - '向天' means 'toward the sky,' and '呵气' means 'exhale.'
  • 气高数丈 - '气高' means 'high' or 'tall,' and '数丈' means 'several feet.'
  • 若匹练上冲 - '若' means 'like,' '匹练' means 'a ribbon,' and '上冲' means 'rising up.'
  • 贵相 - Means 'good fortune' or 'prosperity.'
  • 遂以女妻之 - '遂' means 'then,' '以女' means 'his daughter,' and '妻之' means 'married to him.'
  • 终为将相 - '终' means 'finally,' '为' means 'become,' and '将相' means 'generals and chancellors.'
  • 出独异志 - '出独异志' means 'recorded in the *Du Yi Zhi* (a historical record).'
  • 窦易 - A person's name.
  • 幼时名秘 - '幼时' means 'when young,' and '名秘' means 'named Mi.'
  • 家贫就业村学 - '家贫' means 'poor family,' '就业' means 'to work,' and '村学' means 'village school.'
  • 敎授有道术 - '敎授' means 'teacher,' '有道术' means 'possesses mystical arts.'
  • 人不知 - '人不知' means 'no one knows.'
  • 近暮风雪暴至 - '近暮' means 'near evening,' '风雪' means 'wind and snow,' and '暴至' means 'suddenly arrived.'
  • 学童悉归家不得而宿于漏屋之中 - '学童' means 'schoolchildren,' '悉归家' means 'all returned home,' '不得' means 'unable to,' and '宿于漏屋之中' means 'stayed in a leaking house.'
  • 寒争附火 - '寒' means 'cold,' '争' means 'compete,' and '附火' means 'gather around the fire.'
  • 唯窦公寝于榻 - '唯' means 'only,' '窦公' means 'Mr. Dou,' and '寝于榻' means 'slept on the bed.'
  • 夜深方觉 - '夜深' means 'deep into the night,' and '方觉' means 'finally woke up.'
  • 抚公令起 - '抚' means 'patted,' '公' means 'Mr. Dou,' and '令起' means 'told to get up.'
  • 勉励自爱也 - '勉励' means 'encourage,' '自爱' means 'self-love,' and '也' means 'too' or 'as well.'
  • 德宗幸奉天 - '德宗' is the era name of Emperor Dezong, and '幸奉天' means 'visited Fengtian.'
  • 日公方举进士 - '日' means 'day,' '公' means 'Mr. Dou,' '方举' means 'was just,' and '进士' means 'candidate for the imperial examination.'
  • 随驾而西 - '随驾' means 'followed the emperor,' and '而西' means 'westward.'
  • 乘一蹇驴 - '乘' means 'ride,' '一' means 'one,' '蹇驴' means 'a lame donkey.'
  • 至开逺门 - '至' means 'arrived at,' and '开逺门' means 'the Kaiyuan Gate.'
  • 人稠路隘 - '人稠' means 'crowded with people,' and '路隘' means 'narrow road.'
  • 其扉将阖 - '其扉' means 'the door,' and '将阖' means 'was about to close.'
  • 惧势不可进 - '惧' means 'fear,' '势' means 'situation,' '不可进' means 'could not proceed.'
  • 闻一人叱驴兼捶其后 - '闻' means 'heard,' '一人' means 'a person,' '叱驴' means 'scolded the donkey,' and '捶其后' means 'beat its back.'
  • 顾见一黑衣卒 - '顾见' means 'looked back and saw,' '一' means 'one,' '黑衣' means 'black-clad,' and '卒' means 'soldier.'
  • 呼公曰秀才 - '呼' means 'called,' '公' means 'Mr. Dou,' and '曰秀才' means 'said scholar.'

Proper Nouns

  • 懿安皇后 - The title of the Empress.
  • 宣宗 - The era name of Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 光禄卿 - The title of a high-ranking official in the imperial court.
  • 刑部侍郎 - The title of a deputy minister in the Ministry of Justice.
  • 盐铁使 - The title of an official responsible for salt and iron monopolies.
  • 大理卿 - The title of the chief justice.
  • 长沙 - The name of a city.
  • 奉天 - The name of a place.
  • 开逺门 - The name of a gate.
  • 窦易 - A person's name.
  • 窦秘君 - A person's name.
  • 德宗 - The era name of Emperor Dezong.

[2631]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 畱连月余日 - Stayed for more than a month.
  • 防有过客 - Unexpected visitor.
  • 能相术 - Ability to read faces or fortune-telling.
  • 自上方抵下方日已暮矣 - From the upper part of the temple to the lower part, it was already late in the day.
  • 李先下崔冲后来冲大呼李云待冲来待冲来 - Li went down first, and Cui Chong came later. Cui Chong shouted loudly, 'Wait for me, wait for me.'
  • 君厄过矣 - Your calamity has passed.
  • 若当此际诸公宜早避焉 - If this happens, all of you should avoid it early.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 李潼崔冲二进士 - Li Tong and Cui Chong were both Jinshi (进士), indicating they were successful in the imperial examinations.
  • 滑台节度使贾耽 - Jia Dan was a military governor of Huatai during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 太和末 - The end of the Tahe period (827-835 AD) was a significant time in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 秉钧衡 - Holding the power of the state, a metaphor for holding high political office.
  • 潜匿于山谷间 - Hiding in the mountains and valleys, a common practice for those who wished to avoid political turmoil.

Proper Nouns

  • 李潼 - Li Tong, a Jinshi (进士).
  • 崔冲 - Cui Chong, a Jinshi (进士).
  • 贾耽 - Jia Dan, a military governor of Huatai during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 贾餗 - Jia Fu, a Jinshi (进士) who later held high political office.
  • 太和 - The Tahe period (827-835 AD) of the Tang Dynasty.

[2632]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有异术 - Refers to having extraordinary abilities.
  • 更作何官 - A question asking about future positions or promotions.
  • 适见尚书 - A phrase indicating a recent encounter with someone in a high-ranking position.
  • 尚书画鹢百艘 - Refers to the beauty and grandeur of a scene with painted boats.
  • 人生一世若轻尘之着草 - A metaphor comparing life to dust settling on grass, emphasizing its fleeting nature.
  • 荣悴定分 - Refers to the predetermined fortunes and misfortunes in life.
  • 崔副使刍言 - Refers to the speech or advice given by Cui the Deputy Magistrate.
  • 李推官正范 - Refers to Li the Magistrate, known for his upright and exemplary behavior.
  • 省郎 - Refers to an official in the central government.
  • 郡守团练 - Refers to a local governor and military commander.
  • 判官任毂 - Refers to Ren Gu, a judge or magistrate.
  • 小谏 - Refers to a minor official responsible for advising the ruler.
  • 朱衣 - Refers to a red robe, often associated with high-ranking officials.
  • 杨损支使评事 - Refers to Yang Sun, a subordinate official who serves as an evaluator.
  • 卢判官纁 - Refers to Lu the Judge, known by his surname Xun.
  • 团练李判官 - Refers to Li the Judge, a local military commander.
  • 数场 - Refers to several occasions or events.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 浙东 - Refers to the eastern part of Zhejiang Province.
  • 婺女 - Refers to a region in Zhejiang Province.
  • 观察使 - Refers to a provincial governor or inspector.
  • 尚书 - Refers to a high-ranking official in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 荣悴定分 - A concept from Chinese philosophy and literature, emphasizing the predetermined nature of life's fortunes and misfortunes.
  • 省郎 - Refers to officials in the central government, often involved in administrative and judicial matters.
  • 郡守团练 - Refers to a local governor and military commander, responsible for both civil and military affairs.
  • 判官 - Refers to a judge or magistrate, responsible for legal and administrative duties.
  • 小谏 - Refers to a minor official responsible for advising the ruler, often on moral and ethical matters.
  • 朱衣 - Refers to a red robe, often associated with high-ranking officials and nobility.
  • 评事 - Refers to an evaluator or assessor, often involved in administrative and legal matters.
  • 团练 - Refers to a local military commander, responsible for maintaining order and security.

Proper Nouns

  • 浙东李褒 - Li Bao from Zhe Dong.
  • 婺女娄千寳吕元芳 - Lou Qianbao and Lü Yuanfang.
  • 观察使 - Provincial governor or inspector.
  • 尚书 - High-ranking official in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 崔副使 - Cui the Deputy Magistrate.
  • 李推官 - Li the Magistrate.
  • 判官任毂 - Ren Gu, a judge or magistrate.
  • 小谏 - Minor official responsible for advising the ruler.
  • 杨损支使评事 - Yang Sun, a subordinate official who serves as an evaluator.
  • 卢判官纁 - Lu the Judge, known by his surname Xun.
  • 团练李判官 - Li the Judge, a local military commander.

[2633]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 使与杨李三人禄秩区分矣 - Formal statement meaning 'let the salaries and ranks of Yang, Li, and the third person be distinguished,' setting up the context for the following discussion.
  • 二术士所言咸未之信 - Means 'the words of the two sages were not all believed,' indicating skepticism towards their claims.
  • 是后李服古不过五日而逝 - Means 'after that, Li Fugǔ did not live for more than five days,' indicating a short lifespan.
  • 诚大醉不过数场也 - Means 'indeed, he did not drink more than a few times,' suggesting a limited indulgence in alcohol.
  • 李尚书及诸从事验其所説敬之如神 - Means 'Li Shangshu and his subordinates verified what he said and treated him like a god,' indicating high regard for the sages' predictions.
  • 时罗郎中绍权赴任明州窦余少卿【常之子也】赴台州 - Means 'at that time, Luo Langzhong Shaoquan was appointed to Mingzhou, and Dòu Hóngyú, the Shaoqing [son of Chang], was appointed to Taizhou,' providing a temporal and geographical context.
  • 李公于席上问台明二使君如何娄千寳曰窦使君必当再醉望海亭罗使君此去便应求道四明山不游尘世矣 - Means 'Li Gong asked about the two officials of Taizhou and Mingzhou at the banquet, and Lou Qianbao said that the official of Taizhou would definitely drink again at the Sea-view Pavilion, and the official of Mingzhou will go to the Four Mirrors Mountain to seek the Dao and not return to the mundane world,' setting up a dialogue and introducing predictions about the officials' futures.
  • 后窦少卿罢郡再之府庭是重醉也 - Means 'after Dòu Shaoqing resigned from his post, he returned to the capital, which was a second drinking,' confirming the prediction.
  • 罗郎中迁于海岛故以学道为名知其不还也 - Means 'Luo Langzhong was transferred to an island, so he used seeking the Dao as an excuse, knowing he would not return,' explaining the official's decision.
  • 李尚书归义兴未防物故是无他拜卢纁判官校理 - Means 'Li Shangshu returned to Yixing but died unexpectedly, which was another appointment as Lu Xun's judge and editor,' describing a subsequent event.
  • 明年逝于宛陵使幕比李服古官稍乆矣 - Means 'the next year, he died in Wanling, serving as an official longer than Li Fugǔ,' comparing lifespans and careers.
  • 为少年也任毂判官才为补阙休官归圃田是不至朱紫也 - Means 'as a young man, he served as a judge and then resigned to retire to Pǔtián, not reaching high office,' describing a career path.
  • 崔刍言郎中止于呉兴郡李范郎中止于九江 - Means 'Cui Chuyán Langzhong stopped in Wúxīng Prefecture, and Li Fàn Langzhong stopped in Jiǔjīang,' providing further examples of officials' fates.
  • 二公皆自南宫出为名郡是乃禄秩相参独 - Means 'both officials came from the Nan Gong and served in famous prefectures, their salaries and ranks being comparable,' concluding the discussion.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 杨李三人 - Refers to three individuals named Yang, Li, and a third person, likely officials or notable figures.
  • 术士 - Refers to sages or wise men, often associated with divination and prophecy.
  • 禄秩 - Refers to salary and rank, indicating social and economic status.
  • 大醉 - Refers to heavy drinking, a common theme in classical Chinese literature.
  • 李尚书 - Refers to Li Shangshu, a high-ranking official.
  • 从事 - Refers to subordinates or officials under Li Shangshu.
  • 罗郎中 - Refers to Luo Langzhong, an official.
  • 绍权 - Refers to Luo Langzhong's name or title.
  • 明州 - Refers to a prefecture in Zhejiang Province.
  • 窦余 - Refers to Dòu Hóngyú, an official.
  • 少卿 - Refers to a junior official rank.
  • 常之子也 - Indicates that Dòu Hóngyú is the son of Chang, providing a family background.
  • 台州 - Refers to a prefecture in Zhejiang Province.
  • 李公 - Refers to Li Gong, another official.
  • 台明二使君 - Refers to the officials of Taizhou and Mingzhou.
  • 娄千寳 - Refers to Lou Qianbao, an official or sage.
  • 望海亭 - Refers to a pavilion overlooking the sea, often a place of leisure and contemplation.
  • 四明山 - Refers to a mountain in Zhejiang Province, known for its scenic beauty and spiritual significance.
  • 尘世 - Refers to the mundane world, contrasting with a spiritual or otherworldly realm.
  • 窦少卿 - Refers to Dòu Shaoqing, an official.
  • 府庭 - Refers to the capital or the imperial court.
  • 罗郎中 - Refers to Luo Langzhong, an official.
  • 海岛 - Refers to an island, often a place of isolation or retreat.
  • 学道 - Refers to seeking the Dao, a spiritual or philosophical pursuit.
  • 李尚书 - Refers to Li Shangshu, a high-ranking official.
  • 义兴 - Refers to a place, likely a prefecture or county.
  • 物故 - Refers to death, often used in official documents.
  • 卢纁 - Refers to Lu Xun, an official.
  • 判官 - Refers to a judge or official.
  • 校理 - Refers to an editor or scholar.
  • 宛陵 - Refers to a place, likely a prefecture or county.
  • 毂判官 - Refers to a judge or official.
  • 补阙 - Refers to a position in the imperial court, often a minor official.
  • 休官 - Refers to resignation from office.
  • 圃田 - Refers to a place, likely a garden or rural area.
  • 朱紫 - Refers to high office or noble status.
  • 崔刍言 - Refers to Cui Chuyán, an official.
  • 呉兴郡 - Refers to a prefecture in Zhejiang Province.
  • 李范 - Refers to Li Fàn, an official.
  • 九江 - Refers to a prefecture in Jiangxi Province.
  • 南宫 - Refers to the imperial palace or a high-ranking official's residence.

[2634]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 尚书三十年来两为给事再任京尹 - Describes a long and varied career in government service, with '尚书' (Shangshu) referring to a high-ranking official, '给事' (Gishì) being an advisory role, and '京尹' (Jingyin) indicating a position in the capital city.
  • 防御三峰青州节度使 - Describes a military and administrative role, with '防御' (Fangyu) meaning defense, '三峰' (Sanfeng) possibly referring to a specific location or title, and '青州节度使' (Qingzhou Jiedushi) being a military governor of Qingzhou.
  • 年逾耳顺官歴藩垣 - '年逾耳顺' (Nian yu ershun) means 'past the age of sixty,' and '官歴藩垣' (Guanli fan yuan) refers to a long career in provincial administration.
  • 浙东同院诸公福夀悉不如也 - Compares the longevity and fortune of the subject to other officials, with '福夀' (Fushou) meaning 'good fortune and longevity.'
  • 皆依娄吕二生所说焉 - Indicates reliance on the advice of two scholars, '娄吕二生' (Lou Lü Ersheng).
  • 问千寳已为宰相之事何如 - Asks about the state of affairs related to someone named '千寳' (Qianbao) who has become a chancellor.
  • 如筮得震卦有声而无形也 - Refers to a divination result, with '震卦' (Zhengua) being the Thunder Hexagram from the I Ching, and '有声而无形' (You sheng er wu xing) meaning 'having sound but no form.'
  • 若领大鎭必忧悒成疾可以修禳 - Advises that if one holds a major post, they might become depressed and ill, and rituals can be performed to avert this, with '修禳' (Xiu rang) meaning 'perform rituals to avert misfortune.'
  • 忽有东门骠骑奏以小疵而承防 - Describes a sudden event involving a military official, '骠骑' (Biaoqi), who reports a minor fault, leading to a defensive measure.
  • 蒋伸侍郎拜相 - Indicates that '蒋伸' (Jiang Sheng) was appointed as chancellor.
  • 忧悒不乐去其失望也 - Describes a state of depression and disappointment, with '忧悒' (Youyi) meaning 'depressed' and '失望' (Shiwang) meaning 'disappointed.'
  • 金华娄山人之言果应矣 - Confirms the accuracy of the predictions made by someone from '金华娄山' (Jinhua Lou Shan).
  • 欲令招千寳元芳 - Suggests an intention to invite '千寳元芳' (Qianbao Yuanfang).
  • 寻亦终于郓州 - Indicates that the subject eventually died in '郓州' (Yunzhou).
  • 钟离侑少詹昔岁闲居东越覩斯异术每求之二生不可得也 - Describes a person named '钟离侑' (Zhongli You) who, in his leisure time in '东越' (Dongyue), tried to learn a unique skill from two scholars but was unsuccessful.
  • 出云溪友议 - The title of the text, indicating it is a collection of discussions or opinions.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 尚书 - A high-ranking official in the Chinese bureaucracy.
  • 给事 - An advisory role in the imperial court.
  • 京尹 - A position in the capital city, often responsible for local administration.
  • 防御三峰青州节度使 - A military governor with defensive responsibilities in Qingzhou.
  • 年逾耳顺 - A term from the *Analects* of Confucius, referring to the age of sixty, when one is said to be able to hear all things without anger.
  • 官歴藩垣 - A long career in provincial administration.
  • 浙东 - A region in eastern China.
  • 娄吕二生 - Two scholars or advisors.
  • 千寳 - A person who becomes a chancellor.
  • 筮得震卦 - A divination result from the I Ching, the Thunder Hexagram.
  • 修禳 - Rituals to avert misfortune.
  • 东门骠骑 - A military official.
  • 蒋伸 - A person appointed as chancellor.
  • 忧悒不乐 - A state of depression and dissatisfaction.
  • 失望 - Disappointment.
  • 金华娄山 - A place name, possibly referring to a specific location or a person from that area.
  • 郓州 - A place name, indicating where the subject died.
  • 钟离侑 - A person who tried to learn a unique skill.
  • 东越 - A region in eastern China.
  • 出云溪友议 - The title of the text, indicating it is a collection of discussions or opinions.

Proper Nouns

  • 尚书 - A high-ranking official.
  • 给事 - An advisory role.
  • 京尹 - A position in the capital city.
  • 防御三峰青州节度使 - A military governor.
  • 年逾耳顺 - A term from the *Analects*.
  • 官歴藩垣 - A long career in provincial administration.
  • 浙东 - A region in eastern China.
  • 娄吕二生 - Two scholars.
  • 千寳 - A person who becomes a chancellor.
  • 筮得震卦 - A divination result.
  • 修禳 - Rituals to avert misfortune.
  • 东门骠骑 - A military official.
  • 蒋伸 - A person appointed as chancellor.
  • 忧悒不乐 - A state of depression.
  • 失望 - Disappointment.
  • 金华娄山 - A place name.
  • 郓州 - A place name.
  • 钟离侑 - A person.
  • 东越 - A region in eastern China.
  • 出云溪友议 - The title of the text.

[2635]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 处士 - A scholar-official who refuses to serve in government, often implying a recluse or hermit
  • 驸马 - A title given to the husband of an imperial princess
  • 判盐铁 - To be in charge of salt and iron administration, a significant economic role
  • 宰弼之耗 - Rumors or news about becoming a prime minister or high-ranking official
  • 秉钧持权 - To hold power and control, often used metaphorically for wielding authority
  • 垂箔细看 - To look through a curtain or screen, often used metaphorically for observing closely
  • 入相必矣 - Will definitely enter the government as a high-ranking official
  • 旬月之内 - Within a month or a few months

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 夏侯生有道崇龟重之 - This sentence structure is elliptical, meaning 'the hermit, who was wise, was highly respected by Liu Chonggui.'
  • 发后三千里有不测之事 - This phrase is metaphorical, indicating a significant event or change after a long period.
  • 归阙至中路得疾而终 - The sentence is elliptical, meaning 'on the way back to the capital, he fell ill and died.'
  • 行止事无不验 - The sentence is elliptical, meaning 'all predictions about his actions and fate came true.'
  • 饮啄之有分 - This phrase is elliptical, meaning 'his destiny was predetermined, like the timing of drinking and eating.'
  • 荆南节度判司空董与京兆杜无隐即滑台杜慆常侍之子 - This sentence is complex, meaning 'Dong, the governor of Jingnan, and Du Wu Yin from Jingzhao, the son of the former official Du Tao, were both scholars.'
  • 京兆杜慆常侍之子 - This phrase is elliptical, meaning 'Du Wu Yin is the son of the former official Du Tao from Jingzhao.'
  • 忽为人为絷维 - This phrase is elliptical, meaning 'suddenly, his fate changed.'
  • 二纪依栖竟麻衣也 - This sentence is elliptical, meaning 'he lived in seclusion for a long time, eventually wearing coarse clothing.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 妾欲窮慮盡思 - Formal expression indicating deep contemplation and effort; translated as 'I wish to exhaust my thoughts and efforts'.
  • 視外無有蔽礙 - Descriptive clause indicating a clear and unobstructed view; translated as 'Seeing no obstruction outside'.
  • 列侍者飄然自涼 - Poetic description of a cooling effect; translated as 'The attendants feel a cool breeze as if they are floating'.
  • 若馭風而行也 - Metaphor for a light and effortless experience; translated as 'As if traveling by the wind'.
  • 權稱善 - Indicates approval; translated as 'The Lord praised it'.
  • 夫人乃防發以神膠續之 - Reference to a magical or supernatural material; translated as 'The lady then used divine glue to mend it'.
  • 神膠出郁夷國 - Reference to a mythical place; translated as 'Divine glue comes from the country of Yuyi'.
  • 接弓弩之斷者百斷百續 - Emphasizes the glue's effectiveness; translated as 'It can mend broken bowstrings a hundred times'.
  • 乃織爲羅縠 - Describes the transformation of the material; translated as 'It was then woven into a fine gauze'.
  • 累月而成 - Indicates a lengthy process; translated as 'It took several months to complete'.
  • 裁之爲幔 - Describes the final product; translated as 'It was cut into a curtain'.
  • 内外視之飄飄如煙氣 - Poetic description of the curtain's appearance; translated as 'When viewed from inside and outside, it appears light as smoke'.
  • 輕動而房内自涼 - Describes the curtain's cooling effect; translated as 'When moved slightly, the room becomes cool'.
  • 時權尚在軍旅 - Provides historical context; translated as 'At that time, the Lord was still on military campaigns'.
  • 常以此幔自隨以爲征幕 - Indicates its practical use; translated as 'He always carried this curtain with him as a tent'.
  • 舒之則廣縱數丈 - Describes the curtain's size; translated as 'When unfolded, it is several zhang wide'.
  • 卷之則可内于枕中 - Describes its compactness; translated as 'When rolled up, it can fit inside a pillow'.
  • 時人謂之絲絕 - Indicates its extraordinary quality; translated as 'People at the time called it the 'silk of the gods''.
  • 故呉有三絕四海無儔其妙 - Emphasizes the uniqueness of the curtain; translated as 'Therefore, Wu had three wonders, and none in the world could match its excellence'.
  • 後有貪寵求媚者言夫人多耀于人主因而致退黜 - Indicates a political consequence; translated as 'Later, someone who sought favor and flattery said that the lady was too dazzling for the ruler, leading to her demotion'.
  • 雖見疑墜猶存録其巧工 - Indicates her lasting legacy; translated as 'Although she was suspected and demoted, her craftsmanship was still recorded'.
  • 及呉亡不知所在 - Indicates a historical gap; translated as 'And when Wu fell, her whereabouts were unknown'.
  • 大興中 - Provides historical context; translated as 'During the Great Prosperity period'.
  • 衡陽區純 - Indicates the author and location; translated as 'Xu Chun of Hengyang'.
  • 作防市四方丈余 - Describes the size of the pavilion; translated as 'Made a square market pavilion over ten zhang'.
  • 開四門門有木人縱四五防于中 - Describes the pavilion's structure; translated as 'With four doors, each door had a wooden figure about four or five feet tall'.
  • 欲出門木人輒以椎椎之 - Describes the pavilion's security mechanism; translated as 'When someone tried to leave the door, the wooden figure would hit them with a mallet'.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '刻木爲獭沉于水中取鱼引首而出' describes the function of the wooden otter, which sinks to catch fish and then surfaces. The sentence structure is somewhat elliptical, but the meaning is clear from the context.
  • The sentence '薛眘惑者善投壶龙跃隼飞矫无遗箭置壶于背后却反矢以投之百发百中' describes the skill of Xue Shenhuo in the game of arrow pot. The sentence is highly elliptical, with multiple actions compressed into a single clause.
  • The sentence '马待封能穷伎巧于是指南车记里鼓相风鸟等待封皆改修其巧逾于古待封又爲皇后造妆具中立镜台台下两层皆有门户后将栉沐啓镜奁后台下开门有木妇人手执巾栉至后取已木人即还至于面脂妆粉眉黛髻花应所用物皆木人执继至取毕即还门户复闭如是供给皆木人后既妆罢诸门皆阖乃持去其妆台金银彩画木妇人衣服装饰穷极精妙焉' describes the craftsmanship of Ma Daifeng, detailing his creation of various mechanical devices and the intricate design of the queen's makeup tools. The sentence is highly elliptical and requires careful parsing to understand the sequence of events and the functionality of the devices.
  • The sentence '马待封耻之又奏请造欹器酒山扑满等物许之皆以白银造作其酒山扑满中机关运动或四面' describes Ma Daifeng's request to create additional mechanical devices and the emperor's approval. The sentence is somewhat elliptical, particularly in describing the internal mechanisms of the devices.

  • 洛阳令崔师本又好为古文樗蒱 - The magistrate of Luoyang, Cui Shiben, who enjoyed writing in ancient style and playing the game of dice.
  • 其法三分其子三百六十限以二关人执六马 - The method of dividing the pieces into three parts, with 360 pieces, limited by two checkpoints, and each player controlling six horses.
  • 其骰五枚分上为黒下为白黑者刻二为犊白者刻二为雉 - Five dice, divided into black and white, with black dice having two dots called 'ox' and white dice having two dots called 'pheasant'.
  • 掷之全黑乃为卢其彩十六二雉三黑为雉其彩十四二犊三白为犊其彩十全白为白其彩八四者贵彩也 - If all five dice are black, it is called 'Lu' with a score of 16; two pheasants and three blacks are called 'pheasant' with a score of 14; two oxen and three whites are called 'ox' with a score of 10; all white is called 'white' with a score of 8; these are the high scores.
  • 开为十二塞为十一塔为五秃为四枭为二撅为三 - Opening is 12, blocking is 11, tower is 5, bald is 4, hawk is 2, twist is 3, indicating additional scoring terms.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 终为鄙器 - Phrase meaning 'ultimately becoming a lowly tool,' used to describe the sword's fate despite its extraordinary nature.

  • 杀三牲衅祭之 - Phrase meaning 'to sacrifice three animals to consecrate it,' indicating the ritual purification and dedication of the sword.
  • 世世相传 - Phrase meaning 'passed down through generations,' emphasizing the long history and lineage of the sword.
  • 改其库名曰灵金藏 - Phrase meaning 'to change the name of the treasury to 'Spiritual Gold Treasury,' indicating the renaming of the storage place.
  • 擅权 - Phrase meaning 'to usurp power,' indicating the actions of the Lü clan during the reign of Emperor Hui.
  • 贮禁兵器 - Phrase meaning 'to store prohibited weapons,' indicating the purpose of the treasury after the Lü clan's downfall.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 上皇 - Refers to the First Emperor of Qin, Ying Zheng, also known as the 'First Emperor' or 'Qin Shi Huang.'

  • 诸吕擅权 - Refers to the period when the Lü clan, led by Empress Lü, held significant political power after the death of Emperor Gaozu.
  • 惠帝 - Refers to Emperor Hui of Han, the son of Emperor Gaozu and the first emperor of the Han dynasty to succeed him directly.
  • 灵金藏 - Refers to a treasury named 'Spiritual Gold Treasury,' which was later renamed to reflect the sword's mystical properties.

[2687]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 又漢帝相传以秦王子婴所奉白玉玺 - Phrase introducing the tradition of Han emperors receiving the jade seal from the last Qin prince, Ziying, reflecting the legitimacy of the Han dynasty.
  • 高祖斩白蛇剑 - Refers to the legendary sword of Emperor Gaozu of Han, used to kill a white snake, symbolizing divine right and legitimacy.
  • 剑上皆用七防珠九华玉以为饰 - Compound phrase describing the sword's embellishment with seven types of protective pearls and nine types of ornate jade.
  • 杂厕五色琉璃为剑匣 - Means 'interwoven with multicolored glass to make the sword scabbard.' *厕* here is used in the sense of 'interwoven' or 'mixed in.'
  • 其光景犹照于外与挺剑不殊 - Metaphorical expression meaning the sword's radiance is visible from outside the scabbard, comparable to a bare sword.
  • 十二年一加磨砻 - Fixed expression meaning 'polished once every twelve years.'
  • 刃上常霜雪 - The character  is likely a variant of *凝* (to condense), so this phrase means 'the blade often condenses frost or snow.'
  • 开匣板鞘辄有风气光彩射人 - Compound phrase describing the magical or supernatural effect of the sword: when the scabbard is opened, a wind or aura with light radiates from it, dazzling the person.
  • 出酉阳杂爼 - Citation from the *Youyang Zazuo* (酉阳杂俎), a classical Chinese text of strange stories and anecdotes from the Tang dynasty. *爼* is likely a typo for *俎* (zǔ), but in this context, it is part of the title *Zazuo* (杂俎).
  • 孙氏应瑞图六神鼎者文质精也 - Complex phrase likely from a lost or obscure text, possibly *Sun Shi Ying Rui Tu* (孙氏应瑞图) or *Ying Rui Tu* (应瑞图), which may mean 'Sun's Book of Auspicious Omens.' *六神鼎* refers to six divine cauldrons.
  • 知吉凶知存亡能轻能重能息能行 - Series of parallel clauses describing the supernatural properties of the cauldrons: they can discern fortune and misfortune, life and death, and can be light or heavy, still or moving.
  • 不灼自沸不汲自满 - Set of parallel clauses: the cauldrons can boil without fire, and fill themselves without being filled.
  • 中生五味 - 'Inside it produces five flavors' – likely symbolic of the five basic tastes or the five elements.
  • 王者兴则出衰则去 - Common political metaphor: the cauldrons appear when a virtuous ruler rises to power and disappear when the dynasty declines.
  • 説苑云 - Reference to *Shuo Yuan* (说苑), a classical Chinese text of anecdotes and moral instruction compiled by Liu Xiang of the Han dynasty.
  • 孝武时汾阴人得宝鼎献之甘泉宫 - Historical reference to Emperor Wu of Han, who received a bronze cauldron from a man in Fen County, symbolizing the Mandate of Heaven.
  • 有説则生无説则死 - Conditional phrase from the *Shuo Yuan* text, meaning 'if there is an explanation, he lives; if not, he dies.'
  • 周德者始于天授成于文武显于周公德泽上畅于天下漏三泉 - Complex sentence describing the moral and political virtues of the Zhou dynasty, reaching the springs beneath the earth.
  • 上天报应鼎为周出 - Statement that heaven's retribution caused the cauldron to emerge from the Zhou dynasty.
  • 今汉继 - 'Now the Han dynasty has inherited [the Mandate of Heaven],' implying the legitimacy of the Han dynasty.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 秦王子婴 - Ziying, the last ruler of the Qin dynasty. His surrender of the imperial seal to the Han founder is a key moment in Chinese dynastic transition.
  • 七防珠九华玉 - Rare and likely symbolic or magical materials, possibly representing protection and divine beauty.
  • 五色琉璃 - Multicolored glass, often used in religious or imperial contexts for its aesthetic and symbolic value.
  • 酉阳杂爼 - Reference to *Youyang Zazuo* (酉阳杂俎), a Tang dynasty text of strange tales, anecdotes, and supernatural stories.
  • 孙氏应瑞图 - Lost or obscure text possibly related to auspicious signs and omens, possibly attributed to the Sun family.
  • 六神鼎 - Six divine cauldrons, often used in Chinese political and religious symbolism to represent imperial legitimacy and cosmic harmony.
  • 説苑 - *Shuo Yuan* (说苑), a classical text of moral and historical anecdotes compiled by Liu Xiang during the Western Han dynasty.
  • 汉武帝 - Emperor Wu of Han, one of the most powerful and influential emperors of the Han dynasty, known for his military campaigns and patronage of Confucianism.
  • 周鼎 - The cauldron of the Zhou dynasty, a powerful symbol of legitimacy in ancient China.
  • 漏三泉 - Metaphor for the deep and enduring virtue of the Zhou dynasty, reaching even the springs beneath the earth.
  • 上天报应 - Common Confucian and Daoist concept that heaven rewards virtue and punishes vice.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long sentence '大行变惑罪合至死遂为府君捕逐逃于河渭之间' describes a sequence of events: the crime, the pursuit, and the escape.
  • The sentence '为下邽陈思恭义女餵养甚厚嫁鹦鹉与同乡人柴华' describes a series of actions: becoming an adopted daughter, being well taken care of, and getting married.

  • 寻幽探穴访奇者 - Those who seek the mysterious and explore the unknown.
  • 往往有异义出于言外 - Often there are strange meanings that go beyond the spoken words.
  • 勣疑其精怪 - Ji doubted that they were spirits or monsters.
  • 引手潜后开匣取镜 - He extended his hand, opened the box, and took out the mirror.
  • 镜光出而二人失声 - As the mirror's light emerged, the two people screamed.
  • 俯伏矮者化为龟胡者化为猿 - The shorter one bowed and turned into a turtle, while the exotic-looking one turned into a monkey.
  • 悬镜至晓二身俱殒 - The mirror was hung until dawn, and both bodies perished.
  • 龟身带绿毛猿身带白毛 - Describes the physical appearance of the transformed turtle and monkey.
  • 即入箕山渡颍水歴太和视玉井 - They immediately entered Ji Mountain, crossed the Yi River, and visited the Jade Well.
  • 井傍有池水湛然绿色 - By the well there was a pool of green water.
  • 问樵夫曰此灵湫耳 - He asked a woodcutter, who said it was a sacred spring.
  • 村闾每八节祭之以祈福祐 - The village would hold rituals every eight festivals to pray for blessings.
  • 一祭有阙即池水出黑云大雹浸堤壊阜 - If one ritual was missed, the pool would produce black clouds and large hailstones, flooding the embankments and destroying the land.
  • 勣引镜照之池水沸涌有雷如震 - Ji held up the mirror to reflect on the pool, and the water boiled, with thunder like a great roar.
  • 忽尔池水腾出池中不遗涓滴 - Suddenly, the pool water surged out, leaving not a single drop behind.
  • 可行二百余步水落于地有一鱼可长丈余麄细大于臂首红额白身作靑黄间色无鳞有涎龙形蛇角嘴尖状如 - Describes a fish that appeared after the water receded, with a length of over ten feet, a red forehead, white body, and a mix of green and yellow colors, no scales, and a dragon-like shape with a snake's horn and a sharp beak.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鲟鱼动而有光在于泥水困而不能远去 - Describes a sturgeon moving with light in muddy water, symbolizing being trapped and unable to escape.
  • 失水而无能为耳 - Means 'losing its habitat and thus being unable to act,' referring to the sturgeon's inability to survive out of water.
  • 刃而为炙甚膏有味以充数朝口腹 - Describes the sturgeon being cut and cooked into a fatty, flavorful dish to satisfy the appetite.
  • 游江南将渡广陵子江忽暗云覆水黑风波涌舟子失容虑有覆没 - Describes traveling south and preparing to cross the Yangtze River near Guangling, where dark clouds covered the water, black waves surged, and the boatman was terrified of capsizing.
  • 擕镜上舟照江中数步明朗彻底风云四敛波涛遂息须臾之间逹济天堑跻摄山麴芳岭或攀绝顶或入深洞逢其群鸟环人而噪数熊当路而蹲以镜挥之熊鸟奔骇 - Describes carrying a mirror onto the boat, shining it into the river, and seeing the water become clear for several steps, the clouds and winds gathering, the waves calming, and reaching the other side of the great barrier. Then, climbing Mount She, encountering birds and bears, and using the mirror to scare them away.
  • 是时利涉浙江遇潮出海涛声振吼数百里而 - Indicates the time when crossing the Qiantang River, meeting the outgoing tide, and hearing the waves roaring for hundreds of miles.

  • 涛既近未可渡 - The waves are close and it is not yet safe to cross, where '涛' refers to large waves or surges in the river.
  • 吾辈必葬鱼腹 - Hyperbolic expression meaning certain death if they attempt to cross.
  • 勣出镜照江波 - Ji held up a mirror to reflect the river waves, an unusual and metaphorical action that likely symbolizes a ritual or magical act.
  • 不进屹如云立 - It did not move, standing firm like a cloud, describing the immobility of the object (likely the reflection) in a dramatic way.
  • 四面江水豁开 - The waters of the river parted on all sides, a metaphorical description of the river clearing a path.

  • 与陈永同归 - They returned together with Chen Yong, indicating a shared journey or companionship.

  • 见道士许藏秘 - They met a Taoist named Xu Cangmi, introducing another mystical figure.

Idiomatic Expressions

[2704]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 并于壁孔前死矣 - Literally 'died before the wall hole,' likely referring to a person who perished in a narrow or confined space, possibly a metaphor for a difficult situation.
  • 从此疾愈 - Means 'from this point on, the illness was cured,' indicating recovery after a certain event or action.
  • 其后寻真至庐山 - Means 'later, in search of truth, he went to Mount Lu,' where '寻真' implies a quest for spiritual or philosophical enlightenment.
  • 婆娑数月 - Means 'stayed for several months,' where '婆娑' can also imply a leisurely or contemplative stay.
  • 或栖息长林或露宿草莽 - Means 'sometimes he rested in long forests, sometimes slept in the wild grass,' describing a life of wandering and simplicity.
  • 虎豹接尾豺狼连迹 - Means 'tigers and leopards followed one after another, wolves and jackals trailed behind,' likely a metaphor for danger or perilous situations.
  • 举镜视之莫不窜伏 - Means 'when he raised the mirror to look, all of them fled,' where '镜' (mirror) could symbolize a tool of insight or power.
  • 洞明易道藏往知来 - Means 'profoundly understands the Way of the *Yi* (Book of Changes), knows the past and foresees the future,' indicating deep wisdom.
  • 天下神物必不乆居人间 - Means 'a divine object of the world will not stay long in the human realm,' suggesting the transient nature of extraordinary things.
  • 今宇宙丧乱他乡未必可止 - Means 'now that the world is in chaos, a foreign land may not be a place to stay,' indicating a sense of instability.
  • 吾子此镜尚在足下衞幸速归家乡也 - Means 'your mirror is still with you, return to your hometown quickly,' where '衞' (wei) could be a name or title.
  • 我卿兄厚礼今当舍人间远去 - Means 'I have received generous gifts from your elder brother, now I must leave the human world,' indicating a farewell or departure.
  • 欲得一别卿请早归长安也 - Means 'I wish to bid you farewell, please return to Chang'an early,' where '长安' (Chang'an) is the ancient name for Xi'an.
  • 勣然其言即时北归 - Means 'Ji agreed to his words and immediately returned north,' where '勣' (Ji) is likely a name.
  • 夜梦镜谓勣曰 - Means 'in a dream, the mirror said to Ji,' indicating a supernatural or symbolic encounter.
  • 匣中悲鸣其声纎远俄而渐大 - Means 'the sound of mourning from the box was faint and distant, but soon grew louder,' where '匣' (box) likely contains the mirror.
  • 俄而渐大龙咆虎吼良乆乃定 - Means 'soon it grew louder, like the roar of a dragon and the roar of a tiger, and after a long time it settled,' describing a powerful and dramatic event.
  • 开匣视之即失镜矣 - Means 'when he opened the box to look, the mirror was gone,' indicating the disappearance of the object.

[2705]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 太平廣記 - Classical Chinese encyclopedia, literally meaning 'Record of the Great Peace', which compiles stories and anecdotes from earlier texts.
  • 子部, 小說家類, 異聞之屬 - Classifications within the *Siku Quanshu* (Complete Library in Four Branches of Literature). '子部' refers to the 'Philosophical Works' section; '小說家類' refers to the 'Novels' category; '異聞之屬' refers to the 'Strange Tales' subcategory.
  • 圖緯之學 - Refers to the study of esoteric and divinatory texts, often associated with astrology and cosmology.
  • 捃拾天下遺逸 - Means to gather or collect forgotten or obscure knowledge from around the world.
  • 自書契之始 - Refers to the beginning of written records, implying ancient times.
  • 考驗神怪 - Means to examine or investigate supernatural phenomena.
  • 里閭所説 - Refers to stories told in villages or neighborhoods, implying local folklore.
  • 撰博物志四百卷 - Means to compile a work called *Boshu* (Records of Marvelous Things) in four hundred volumes.
  • 卿才十倍萬代博識無倫 - A compliment praising Zhang Hua's exceptional knowledge, meaning 'Your talent is tenfold that of any scholar throughout the ages, unparalleled.'
  • 記事采言多所浮妄 - Means that the records and sayings collected contain many unfounded or exaggerated elements.
  • 宜刪翦無據以見成文 - Suggests that Zhang Hua should remove unsupported claims to create a more reliable text.
  • 將繁於耳目也可更芟截浮疑 - Suggests that the text might be too extensive for readers, so it should be shortened and simplified.
  • 分為十卷 - Means to divide the work into ten volumes.
  • 御前賜靑鐵砚 - Refers to an iron inkstone given by the emperor, symbolizing imperial favor.
  • 此鐵是于闐國所獻 - Indicates that the iron was a gift from the kingdom of Khotan (于闐).
  • 麟角管 - Refers to a writing brush made from the horn of a mythical qilin (Chinese unicorn), symbolizing auspiciousness.
  • 此遼西國所獻也 - Indicates that the brush was a gift from the region of Liaoxi.
  • 側理紙萬番 - Refers to a type of paper made from sea algae, known as 'ze li' paper, which was presented in ten thousand sheets.
  • 南越所獻也 - Indicates that the paper was a gift from the southern kingdom of Nanyue.
  • 漢言陟厘陟厘與側理相亂 - Explains that '陟厘' and '側理' were similar terms in ancient Chinese, both referring to the same type of paper.
  • 南人以海苔為紙其理縱横斜側因為名焉 - Describes how the paper was made from seaweed and named for its texture, which was irregular and varied.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太平廣記 - A monumental collection of stories and anecdotes compiled during the Song Dynasty, based on earlier works.
  • 張華 - A famous scholar and official during the Western Jin Dynasty, known for his encyclopedic knowledge.
  • 晉惠帝 - The emperor of the Western Jin Dynasty, whose reign was marked by political instability.
  • 許遜 - A legendary figure associated with Taoism, particularly known for his role in founding the Dragon Tiger Sect.
  • 陶眞白 - A historical figure, though less well-known than others in the list.
  • 張祖宅 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 唐儀 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 唐中宗 - The emperor of the Tang Dynasty, known for his brief reign and political intrigue.
  • 宋青春 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 武勝之 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 李守泰 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 陳仲躬 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 曹王臯 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 漁人 - A reference to a fisherman, often used in classical literature to symbolize simplicity or wisdom.
  • 于闐國 - An ancient kingdom located in present-day Xinjiang, known for its trade and cultural exchanges.
  • 遼西國 - An ancient region in present-day northeastern China, known for its contributions to Chinese culture.

  • The long sequence '妾非杀人者此井有毒龙自汉朝绛侯居于兹遂穿此井' describes the woman's explanation, which must be read as a single narrative.
  • The complex sentence '缘与太一左右侍龙相得每为防蔽天命追征多托故不赴集好食人血自汉以来杀三千七百人矣而水不耗涸' describes the dragon's behavior and its impact, which must be read as a single narrative.
  • The long sequence '某乃国初方坠于井遂为龙所驱使为妖惑以诱人用供龙所食甚于辛苦情所非愿' describes the woman's experience, which must be read as a single narrative.
  • The sequence '昨为太一使者交替天下龙神尽须集驾昨夜子时已朝太一矣兼为河南旱勘责三数日方回' describes the recent events, which must be read as a single narrative.
  • The long sequence '今井内已无水君子诚能命匠淘之则获脱斯难矣若然愿终君子一生奉飬世间之事无不致言讫便失所在' describes the woman's final advice and her disappearance, which must be read as a single narrative.

  • 密竹篮中 - 'In a tightly woven bamboo basket,' referring to the container used for washing the shrimp.
  • 以水淋洗虾子在虾腹下赤如覆盆子 - 'With water to rinse; the shrimp eggs are beneath the shrimp's belly, red like *fupenzi* (a type of berry).'
  • 随水从篮目中下 - 'They flow with the water through the mesh of the basket.'
  • 通计虾一石可得子五升 - 'In total, from one *dan* (stone) of shrimp, five *sheng* (units) of eggs can be obtained.'
  • 缝布作小袋子如径寸半竹大长二尺 - 'Stitch cloth into small bags, the diameter about half an inch, the length two feet.'
  • 以虾子满之急系头 - 'Fill them with shrimp eggs and tie the top tightly.'
  • 随袋多少以末盐封之周厚数寸 - 'Depending on the size of the bag, seal it with fine salt, surrounding it with several inches of thickness.'
  • 经一日夜出晒夜则平板压之 - 'After one day and night, expose it to the sun; at night, press it flat with a plate.'
  • 明旦又出晒夜依前压十日干则拆破袋出虾子梃色如赤琉璃光 - 'The next morning, again expose it to the sun; at night, press it as before. After ten days of drying, break the bags open and the shrimp eggs are rods in color like red glass.'

  • 龙楼李丞相 - Refers to the Chancellor of the Dragon Pavilion, a metaphorical title for a high-ranking official.
  • 昔岁仰高文 - Means 'in the past, admired his high literary talent.'
  • 黄阁今无主 - Means 'the Yellow Hall now has no master,' symbolizing the absence of a great leader.
  • 青山竟不焚 - Means 'the green mountains were never burned,' a metaphor for enduring peace or stability.
  • 尝有徴聘之意 - Means 'there was once an intention to recruit him,' indicating interest in his talents.
  • 唐末以左拾遗授之 - Means 'at the end of the Tang Dynasty, he was appointed as a Left Censor,' a minor but respected official position.
  • 诏下之日疾终于家 - Means 'on the day the imperial edict was issued, he died at home,' a poignant ending.
  • 与皮日休为诗友 - Means 'was a poetic friend with Pi Rihou,' indicating a literary partnership.
  • 顔给事荛 - Refers to Yan Rao, a government official.
  • 谪官殁于湖外 - Means 'exiled and died outside of Lake Tai,' indicating his fate.
  • 未间自草墓志 - Means 'did not have time to write his own epitaph,' suggesting a hurried death.
  • 性躁急不能容物 - Means 'temperamental and impatient, unable to tolerate others,' describing his personality.
  • 寓于东吴 - Means 'lived in Eastern Wu,' referring to the region.
  • 与吴郡陆龟为诗文之交 - Means 'was a literary friend with Wuzhou's Lu Guimeng,' indicating a close relationship.
  • 一纪无渝 - Means 'remained unchanged for a decade,' indicating a long-lasting friendship.
  • 情礼不缺 - Means 'did not lack in emotional and ritual propriety,' showing his respect for traditions.
  • 谏议大夫高公丞之 - Refers to Gao Gong, a former Counselor-in-Advise.
  • 故丞相陆公扆 - Refers to Lu Gong Yi, a former Chancellor.
  • 面交皆如防手过市见利即解防而去 - Means 'all face-to-face friends are like hands that pass by the market and remove their gloves upon seeing profit,' criticizing superficial friendships.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 漢昭帝元始元年 - First year of Emperor Zhao of Han's reign, marking the historical context.
  • 穿淋池廣千歩 - Describes the construction of a pool called Linchi that is a thousand steps wide.
  • 中植分枝荷一茎四叶状如骈葢 - Describes a lotus plant with four leaves growing from one stem, resembling a double canopy.
  • 日照则叶低防根若葵之卫足也 - Describes how the leaves of the lotus plant droop under the sun, with the roots resembling the protective feet of the sunflower.
  • 名曰低光荷 - The lotus is named Diguanghe (Low Light Lotus).
  • 花叶杂萎芬芳之气彻十余里 - Describes the fragrance of the flowers and leaves spreading for over ten miles.
  • 食之令人口气常香益人肌理 - Describes the effect of eating the lotus, which is said to make one's breath fragrant and improve the texture of the skin.
  • 宫人贵之每游宴出入皆含咀或剪以为衣或折以蔽日 - Describes how palace women value the lotus, using it in various ways during outings and banquets.
  • 相为戏楚词谓折芰荷以为衣意在斯也 - Refers to a playful association with the Chuci (Songs of Chu), where lotus is used to make clothes.
  • 又有倒生菱茎如乱丝一花十叶根浮水上实沉泥里泥如紫色谓之紫泥菱食之令人不老 - Describes another aquatic plant, the Dao Sheng Ling, with a stem that grows upside down, resembling tangled threads, and with ten leaves per flower. The roots float on the water, and the fruit sinks into the mud, which is purple and called Zini Ling. Eating this plant is said to prevent aging.
  • 时命水戏游宴永日 - Describes the enjoyment of water-based activities and banquets for long periods.
  • 工人进一巨槽帝曰桧楫松舟嫌其重朴况乎此槽岂可得而乘也 - Describes a craftsman presenting a large boat, which the emperor finds too heavy and bulky, questioning how it could be used.
  • 乃命文梓为舟木兰为枻刻飞鸾翔鹢饰其船首随风轻漾毕景忘归乃至通夜 - Describes the emperor ordering the construction of a light boat with elegant decorations, such as carved flying cranes and egrets, which he enjoys so much that he forgets to return until late at night.
  • 使宫人为歌歌曰商秋素景泛洪波谁云好手折芰荷凉风凄凄揭棹歌云光开曙月低河万岁为乐岂为多 - Describes the emperor commissioning palace women to sing a song about the beauty of autumn and the lotus, expressing his enjoyment of the scene.
  • 帝大悦起游商台于池上及乎末岁谏者多遂省游荡奢侈堙毁台池鸾舟荷芰随时废灭今台址无遗池亦平焉 - Describes the emperor's enjoyment of the Shangtai pavilion by the pool, but later, due to many advisors' warnings, he reduces his extravagant activities, leading to the destruction of the pavilion and the pool, and the disappearance of the lotus boats and plants.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 霄游宫 - A palace built by Emperor Cheng of Han for nighttime activities, often associated with secrecy and luxury.
  • 漆为柱 - Literally 'painted with lacquer,' indicating the use of lacquer to cover the columns, possibly for both aesthetic and protective purposes.
  • 黑绨之幕 - 'Black silk curtains,' where *绨* refers to a type of coarse silk fabric.
  • 器服乘舆皆尚黑色 - 'All vessels, clothing, and vehicles favored black,' indicating a preference for the color black in all aspects of the palace.
  • 悦于暗行 - 'Enjoyed walking in the dark,' suggesting a preference for activities conducted in low light.
  • 憎灯烛之照 - 'Hated the illumination of lamps and candles,' indicating a dislike for bright lights.
  • 衣佩虽加锦绣更以木兰纱绡罩之 - 'Even if the clothes were adorned with brocade and embroidery, they were covered with magnolia silk and gauze,' suggesting a layered and intricate style of dress.
  • 至霄游宫方秉炬烛宴幸 - 'Only when reaching the Xiu You Palace did they hold torches and candles for feasts and visits,' indicating the controlled use of light for special occasions.
  • 好夕出游造飞行殿 - 'Enjoyed evening outings to the flying pavilion,' indicating a preference for nighttime activities and the use of a special vehicle.
  • 一名云雷宫 - 'Also known as the Cloud and Thunder Palace,' providing an alternative name for the palace.
  • 所行之处咸以毡绨借地 - 'Wherever it went, it laid down felt and silk to protect the ground,' indicating the care taken to avoid damage.
  • 恶车辙马迹之喧也 - 'Disliked the noise of wheel ruts and horse tracks,' suggesting a preference for quiet and controlled movement.
  • 国之穷老皆呼万岁 - 'The poor and elderly of the country all shouted 'Long live the emperor!'' indicating widespread support and celebration.
  • 是以鸿嘉永始之间国富家丰兵戈长戢 - 'Therefore, during the *Hong Jia* and *Yong Shi* periods, the country was prosperous, families were wealthy, and there was long peace,' summarizing the positive impact of the emperor's policies.
  • 故刘向谷永窃言指谏 - 'Therefore, Liu Xiang and Gu Yong secretly advised,' indicating that scholars provided advice to the emperor.
  • 于是焚宵游飞行之殿罢宴逸之乐 - 'Thus, the Xiu You Palace and the flying pavilion were burned down, and the feasts and leisure activities were stopped,' describing the consequences of the advice.

  • The phrase '盛夏则清凉自至' describes the cooling effect of the canopy during summer. The seasonal context should be maintained.
  • The phrase '其色隐隐' describes the subtle color of the canopy. The adverbial modifier should be carefully translated to convey the sense of subtlety.
  • The phrase '谓载卧内有紫气' indicates that people believed there was a purple aura in Yuan Zai's bedroom. The belief in the aura adds a mystical element.
  • The phrase '其余服玩奢僭，率皆拟于帝王家' describes the luxurious and presumptuous nature of the other decorations and furnishings. The comparison to the imperial household should be emphasized.
  • The phrase '芸辉堂前有池，以文石砌其岸' describes the pond in front of the hall and the construction of its banks. The descriptive language should be preserved.

  • 及审听之乃芙蓉中也 - 'Upon carefully listening, he found it was coming from within the lotus.' '审听' means to listen carefully, and '乃' indicates realization.
  • 俯而视之闻喘息之音 - 'He looked down and heard the sound of breathing.' This is a dramatic description of what follows.
  • 载大恶逐剖其花一无所见 - 'He then greatly disliked it and cut open the flower, but saw nothing.' '载' again functions as an adverb, and '恶逐' is a compound verb meaning 'to hate and chase.'
  • 因秘不令人説及 - 'Thus, he kept it secret and forbade anyone from speaking of it.' '秘' means to keep secret, and '不令人説及' is a prohibition.
  • 载受戮而逸奴为平卢军卒人故得其实 - 'Eventually, he was killed, and the servant escaped. The servant was a soldier of the Pinglu army, and thus learned the truth.' This sentence is complex and involves a chain of events.
  • 载龙髯拂紫色如烂椹可长三尺 - 'It had a dragon’s beard, purple in color, like a rotten mulberry, and could be three feet long.' '龙髯' is a metaphor for a long, flowing hair or filament, possibly from a mythical creature.
  • 削水晶以为柄刻红玉以为环钮 - 'It was carved from crystal for the handle and red jade for the ring and knob.' This describes the craftsmanship of the object.
  • 置之于堂中夜则蚊蚋不能近 - 'When placed in the hall, at night, mosquitoes and gnats could not approach it.' This is a magical or supernatural property.
  • 拂之为声则鸡犬牛马无不惊逸 - 'When touched, it made a sound that caused chickens, dogs, oxen, and horses to all be startled and flee.' This shows the object’s supernatural power.
  • 若垂之于池潭则鳞甲之属悉俯伏而至 - 'If hung over a pond or pool, all fish and scaled creatures would bow and come.' This is another magical property.
  • 引水于空中即成瀑布长三五尺而未尝辄断 - 'If water was drawn from the air, it would form a waterfall three to five feet long that never ceased.' This is a fantastical description of the object’s power.
  • 烧燕肉薰之则防防焉若生云雾 - 'If the meat of a swallow was burned and used to perfume it, it would emit a soft, mist-like vapor.' This is a description of the object’s fragrance or aura.
  • 厥后上知其异载不得已而进内 - 'Later, the emperor learned of its strange nature and was forced to bring it into the palace.' '厥后' means 'afterward' or 'later,' and '不得已' means 'unwillingly' or 'under duress.'
  • 载自云得之于洞庭道士张知和 - 'He said he had obtained it from the Taoist master Zhang Zhihe of Dongting Lake.' '载自云' is a phrase indicating the object’s own statement or the source of the information.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 玉树后庭花 - A classical Chinese song or piece of music, often associated with the last days of the Southern Dynasties and the decadence of the court.
  • 芙蓉 - 'Lotus' or 'water lily,' often used in Chinese poetry and legend to symbolize purity or a mystical setting. In this context, it may refer to a lotus flower that contains a supernatural object.
  • 龙髯 - 'Dragon’s beard,' a term used in Chinese mythology to describe the long, flowing hair of a dragon or a mystical being. It may also refer to a long, flowing filament or appendage of a magical object.
  • 水晶 - 'Crystal,' a material often associated with purity and magical properties in classical Chinese texts.
  • 红玉 - 'Red jade,' a type of precious stone in Chinese tradition, often used in descriptions of magical or precious objects.
  • 平卢军卒 - 'Soldier of the Pinglu Army.' The Pinglu Army was a military force in the Tang dynasty, stationed in the region of modern-day Hebei.
  • 洞庭 - 'Dongting Lake,' a famous lake in Hunan province, often associated with Daoist mysticism and legendary figures in Chinese mythology.
  • 张知和 - Zhang Zhihe, a Daoist master and legendary figure associated with Dongting Lake. He is a figure from Chinese legend and Daoist tradition, often depicted as a recluse with supernatural knowledge.

  • 王氏父母未知或知亲属以载夫妻皆乞儿厌薄之甚 - Wang's parents either did not know or knew but still looked down on Yuan Zai and Wang Yunsui, considering them beggars.
  • 元遂游秦为诗别韫秀 - Yuan Zai went to Qin (a region) and wrote a poem to bid farewell to Wang Yunsui.

  • 路扫饥寒迹天哀志气人休淋离别泪防手入西秦 - Wang Yunsui encourages Yuan Zai to leave, suggesting that they should not be burdened by the hardships of life and should part ways amicably.
  • 广葺亭台 - Building and expanding pavilions and terraces, which was a common practice for wealthy officials to display their status.

  • 韫秀少有识量节槩亦高 - Character description; '韫秀' (a name, possibly a woman) and '少有识量节槩' (had foresight, measure, and integrity from a young age) suggest a virtuous character.
  • 载被戮上令入宫备彤管箴规之任 - Role in the imperial court; '载被戮' (Zai was executed) and '上令入宫' (the emperor ordered her to enter the palace) and '备彤管箴规之任' (to serve as a counselor with a red pen) describe her duties.
  • 王家十二娘子二十年太原节度使女十六年宰相妻 - Background information; '王家十二娘子' (the twelfth daughter of the Wang family) and '二十年太原节度使女' (daughter of a military governor for twenty years) and '十六年宰相妻' (wife of a chancellor for sixteen years) provide her background.
  • 谁能书得长信昭阳之事死亦幸矣 - Historical sensitivity; '长信昭阳' (Changxin and Zhaoyang) are historical figures or places, and '死亦幸矣' (it would be fortunate to die) suggests a wish to avoid writing about these matters.
  • 坚不从命 - Determination; '坚不从命' (firmly refused to comply) shows her determination.
  • 或云上宥其罪或云京兆笞而毙之 - Conflicting accounts; '或云' (some say) introduces uncertainty, and '上宥其罪' (the emperor pardoned her) and '京兆笞而毙之' (the capital officials beat her to death) present conflicting accounts.
  • 载宠姬薛瑶英能诗书善歌舞仙姿玉质肌香体轻 - Talents and beauty; '载宠姬' (Zai's favorite concubine) and '薛瑶英' (name) and '能诗书善歌舞' (skilled in poetry, calligraphy, and dance) and '仙姿玉质肌香体轻' (divine appearance, jade-like quality, fragrant skin, and light body) describe her talents and beauty.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 逮夜有投书于其戸者 - This clause is elliptical but means 'one night, a letter was thrown onto his door.'
  • 为其先考所遗者 - This phrase modifies the letter, indicating it was left by his late father.
  • 汝之获利吾之防助也 - This clause explains the relationship between the young man's profit and the letter's message.
  • 今将有大祸然吾已请于隂隲矣 - This concessive clause acknowledges an impending disaster but also states that the spirits have been asked.
  • 汝及朔旦宜斋躬洁服 - This imperative clause gives specific instructions for the young man to follow.
  • 出于春明门外逆旅备缣帛 - This clause describes the young man's actions in preparation for the ritual.
  • 俟夜分往防水桥歩及石岸 - This clause outlines the timing and location of the ritual.
  • 见黄衣者即置于前礼祝而退 - This conditional clause describes the ritual steps if the young man encounters someone in yellow.
  • 或无所遇即挈缣以归 - This alternative clause describes what to do if no one is encountered.
  • 急理家事当为窜计 - This imperative clause advises the young man to prepare to flee.
  • 祸不旋踵矣 - This clause emphasizes the immediacy of the disaster.
  • 捧书大恐合室素服而泣 - This clause describes the emotional state of the young man and his family.
  • 专志朔旦则舍弃他事弹冠振衣 - This clause describes the young man's focused preparation.
  • 止于春明门外 - This clause describes the young man's location.
  • 矜严不寐恭俟夜分 - This clause describes the young man's demeanor and actions.
  • 乃从一仆乘一马驰往防桥 - This clause describes the young man's journey.
  • 唯恐无所覩 - This clause expresses the young man's fear.
  • 至则果有一物形质诡怪 - This clause describes the encounter with the strange creature.
  • 蓬头黄衣交臂束膝柱而坐俛首以寐 - This clause provides a detailed description of the creature.
  • 少年惊喜捧缣于前祈祝设拜不敢郤顾 - This clause describes the young man's actions and reactions.
  • 疾驱而囘返辕相庆以为幸免矣 - This clause describes the young man's return and celebration.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 裴延龄 - A real historical figure from the Tang dynasty, known for his role in finance and his reputation for corruption and manipulation of official records.
  • 司農少卿 - Assistant Minister of Agricultural Affairs, one of the key officials in the Tang government responsible for managing state granaries, land, and agricultural production.
  • 度支 - The Department of Finance, responsible for managing state revenues, expenditures, and the allocation of resources.
  • 左藏库 - The Left Treasure Warehouse, one of the main state treasuries in the Tang dynasty, used for storing government funds and goods.
  • 季库月给 - Refers to seasonal or monthly allocations of goods and money, likely for distribution to officials or military.
  • 宰臣 - Refers to the chief minister or prime minister, often the *Chancellor* or *Shangshu Ling* in the Tang bureaucracy.
  • 京西 - The western part of the capital city, likely referring to the area around Chang’an (modern Xi’an), the capital of the Tang dynasty.
  • 長安咸陽兩縣 - The two counties of Chang’an and Xianyang, which were part of the administrative structure around the capital.

Proper Nouns

  • 裴延龄 - Proper name of the historical figure. Should be consistently rendered as 'Pei Yanling.'
  • 司農少卿 - Title of an official. Should be translated as 'Assistant Minister of Agricultural Affairs.'
  • 度支 - Department of Finance. Should be rendered as 'Department of Finance' or 'Finance Office.'
  • 左藏库 - The Left Treasure Warehouse. Should be translated as 'Left Treasure Warehouse.'
  • 季库月给 - Seasonal or monthly allocations. Should be rendered as 'seasonal and monthly allocations.'
  • 宰臣 - Chief minister or prime minister. Should be translated as 'the prime minister' or 'the chief minister.'
  • 京西 - The western part of the capital. Should be translated as 'the western part of the capital.'
  • 長安咸陽兩縣 - Two counties of Chang’an and Xianyang. Should be translated as 'the two counties of Chang’an and Xianyang.'

  • 至于回纥马价用一分钱物 - Even the payment for horses from the Uighurs uses one part of the tax revenue. '回纥' refers to the Uighur people, and '马价' means 'horse price' or 'payment for horses.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 仪早有才名弟液涤及从兄涖并有文翰 - This sentence lists Yi's family members and their literary talents, requiring careful reading to ensure clarity.
  • 每私宴之际自比王谢之家 - The comparison to the Wang and Xie families is a key point, and the sentence structure must be clear to convey the pride and ambition.
  • 吾之门地及出身歴官未尝不为第一 - The sentence emphasizes Yi's pride in his family background and career, and the structure must be preserved to maintain the tone.
  • 丈夫当先据要路以制人岂能默默受制于人 - The rhetorical question and the contrast between taking control and being controlled must be clear.
  • 故进取不已而不以令终 - The sentence structure indicates a relentless pursuit of success, even if it leads to an untimely end.
  • 又湜谄事张易之与韦庶人及韦诛复附太平 - The sentence describes Shi's opportunistic behavior, and the sequence of events must be clear.
  • 有冯子都董偃之宠 - The sentence refers to two historical figures known for their close relationship with the imperial court.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 少主 - Refers to a young ruler, typically a young emperor.
  • 尧有敢谏之鼓舜有诽谤之木 - Refers to the mechanisms established by Emperors Yao and Shun to encourage officials and subjects to offer criticism and advice.
  • 承祧秉禄 - Inheriting the throne and holding power, signifying the emperor's legitimacy and authority.
  • 许昌发迹阆苑起身 - The late emperor Xu Chang began his career in Langyuan, indicating the starting point of his rise to power.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '密呈少主少主覩之不觉心狂遂决幸秦之计' is long and needs clarification on the relationship between '密呈少主' and '覩之不觉心狂', as well as the decision-making process in '遂决幸秦之计'.
  • The sentence '因下制曰朕闻前王巡狩观土地之惨舒历代省方慰元之傒望西秦封域远在边隅先皇帝画此山河历年征讨虽归王化未浃惠风今耕稼既属有年军民颇闻望幸用安疆塲聊议省巡' is long and requires clear logical relationships, especially between '因下制曰' and the subsequent content.
  • The sentence '选取今年十月三日幸秦州布告中外咸使闻知由是中外切谏不从母后泣而止之以至绝食' is long and needs to clarify the causal relationship between '选取今年十月三日幸秦州' and '由是中外切谏不从'.
  • The sentence '前秦州节度判官蒲禹卿叩马泣血上表谏曰臣闻尧有敢谏之鼓舜有诽谤之木汤有司过之士周有诫慎之鼗盖古者明君克全帝道欲知已过要纳谠言将引咎而责躬庶理人而修德陛下自承祧秉禄正位当天爱闻逆耳之忠言每犯颜而谏且先皇帝许昌发迹阆苑起身' is long and requires clear logical relationships, especially between '上表谏曰' and the subsequent content.

Proper Nouns

  • 少主 - Young ruler.
  • 前王 - Former kings.
  • 尧 - Ancient legendary emperor, known for his benevolence.
  • 舜 - Ancient legendary emperor, known for his virtue.
  • 汤 - Founder of the Shang dynasty.
  • 周 - Zhou dynasty.
  • 许昌 - Name of the late emperor.
